title: Marco Rubio calls India ‘great partner,’ pushes for stronger energy ties
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio expressed the United States' desire to expand energy ties with India, highlighting historic US production and export levels. Ahead of his visit, Rubio emphasized India's role as a "great partner" and discussed potential opportunities, including Venezuelan oil, to address energy price concerns.

title: Will Dhoni play in IPL 2027? CSK captain Gaikwad gives 'next year' update
content: MS Dhoni's participation in IPL 2027 is uncertain. Chennai Super Kings captain Ruturaj Gaikwad stated that even the team is unaware of Dhoni's return next season. Dhoni missed IPL 2026 due to injury. CSK's campaign ended with a loss to Gujarat Titans. The team faced challenges with injuries and a young squad.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Venezuela acting president to visit India next week for oil discussions: Rubio
content: Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodríguez is set to visit India next week for oil sales discussions, as the US aims to bolster energy ties with New Delhi. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio highlighted India as a key partner, expressing readiness to increase energy exports. This comes as Venezuela, possessing vast oil reserves, seeks to diversify its energy market.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: Delhi double murder: Woman, teen son stabbed to death during robbery
content: A shocking double murder has occurred in Govindpuri, Delhi. A 38-year-old woman, Sharda Sahu, and her 13-year-old son were found stabbed to death. The incident took place in the early hours of Thursday. Police suspect a robbery as cash and jewellery are missing. Investigations are underway to apprehend the culprits.

title: Jamie Dimon in China: To reduce our jobs down the road, we will hire fewer ...
content: JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon predicts artificial intelligence will reduce some banking jobs.  The bank plans to hire more AI specialists and fewer traditional bankers.  This shift will be managed through employee turnover and retraining.  Other global banks also see AI impacting support roles and back-office operations.  The pace of change requires careful societal consideration.

title: Dianna Russini: 'Fighting over him' claim about Sean McVay resurfaces NFL past
content: Sean McVay and Dianna Russini found themselves back in NFL headlines after reporter Robert Littal shared a viral claim connected to McVay’s Commanders days. The comment quickly spread across social media and sparked fresh discussion around the Rams coach’s early career in Washington. While neither McVay nor Russini addressed the rumor publicly, the story gained attention across NFL circles. The buzz also arrived during a busy offseason for McVay as he prepares the Rams for the 2026 season following recent comments about Jared Goff, Matthew Stafford and his biggest coaching regrets.

title: Conor McGregor quote of the day: 'This is hard work. This is an obsession'
content: Conor McGregor built his career around the idea that obsession matters more than talent, rising from welfare payments in Dublin to become the UFC’s biggest global star. As he prepares for another comeback against Max Holloway, McGregor’s story remains less about natural gifts than relentless preparation, psychological conviction and the refusal to accept ordinary limitations.

title: Carlos Alcaraz’s net worth: Endorsements, earnings & luxury lifestyle
content: Carlos Alcaraz has achieved a remarkable career by 22, winning the 2026 Australian Open to complete his Career Grand Slam. His estimated net worth in 2026 ranges from $50 million to $85 million, fueled by over $60 million in prize money and lucrative endorsements from brands like Nike and Rolex.

title: Kangana Ranaut steps out wearing mangalsutra, green bangles; fans curious
content: Kangana Ranaut has once again grabbed the internet’s attention after a recent video of the actress-politician surfaced online. In the now-viral clip, Kangana was seen wearing a mangalsutra and green bangles, leaving fans speculating whether she had secretly tied the knot.

title: 'No NEET paper leak through NTA system', testing agency chief tells MPs
content: NTA chief Abhishek Singh told a parliamentary panel the NEET-UG 2026 paper wasn't leaked via their system, with the CBI investigating. Officials presented reforms, noting 70% of Radhakrishnan Committee recommendations are implemented. The agency faces a 25% staff shortage and is considering computer-based testing for future exams.

title: Titans too tall: CSK knocked out as GT's top-order machine rolls on
content: Shubman Gill scored 64 off 37 balls while B Sai Sudharsan made 84 off 53 balls as the opening pair added 125 runs. It was their seventh century partnership in the IPL, the most by any pair in the tournament’s history.

title: AI-AI-YO! America's new revolt against Big Tech
content: Graduates across America are booing speakers who mention artificial intelligence. This reaction stems from worries about automation impacting jobs and the perception that AI benefits the wealthy. Communities are also protesting data centers due to environmental concerns. The backlash highlights a growing public skepticism about technological progress outpacing democratic consent.

title: Horoscope: Daily astrological predictions for May 22
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 22, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'Not essential part of Eid': Calcutta HC refuses to stay curbs on cattle slaughter
content: The Calcutta High Court has upheld the West Bengal government's restrictions on slaughtering certain animals ahead of Bakr Eid. The court found the notification aligned with previous directives, emphasizing that cow sacrifice isn't a mandatory Islamic practice. Authorities must now decide on exemptions within 24 hours, ensuring proper certification and infrastructure for any permitted slaughter.

title: East Bengal end 22-year wait, lift national title by winning ISL
content: East Bengal ended a 22-year wait for a national league title by clinching the 2025-26 ISL crown after a dramatic final matchday involving five title contenders. Dominant throughout the season, the Red and Gold Brigade combined attacking flair, consistency and resilience to edge rivals Mohun Bagan and script a historic triumph.

title: MS Dhoni misses entire IPL 2026 league stage for CSK, a first in tournament history
content: Mahendra Singh Dhoni's streak of playing in every IPL season for Chennai Super Kings ended in IPL 2026 due to a thumb injury. He missed the final league match against Gujarat Titans, marking his first absence from a game for the franchise he captained to five titles. His return hinges on CSK's playoff qualification.

title: Trump Says Sending 5,000 US Troops To Poland
content: Trump said the move was based on his relationship with Poland's president Karol Nawrocki, a nationalist ally whom he said he was "proud to endorse" in elections that Nawrocki won last year.

title: Umar Khalid Moves Delhi High Court Seeking Interim Bail
content: Umar Khalid had earlier been granted interim bail on several occasions, including for short durations in 2022, 2024 and 2025, and had surrendered on time each time.

title: Delhi Public Transport Drivers' Strike Begins With Partial Shutdown
content: At Ramlila Ground and Anand Vihar, Kashmere Gate Bus Terminus in central Delhi, rows of stationary trucks and autorickshaws stood like silent sentinels under the harsh May sun.

title: Faridabad Couple Arrested For Defrauding People Of Rs 31 Crore: Cops
content: Faridabad police arrested a husband and wife from Mumbai for defrauding people of around Rs 31 crore by promising investments in forex trading and film production, police said on Thursday.

title: NEET Leak Not Through National Testing Agency System, Its Chief Tells MPs
content: The CBI has so far arrested nine accused in this case, including individuals from Jaipur and Sikar in Rajasthan, Gurugram in Haryana, and Nashik, Pune, and Ahilyanagar in Maharashtra.

title: 85% Bandra East Demolition Work Complete Amid Violence, Court Battle
content: A 150-strong mob threw stones at police and demolition officials and used cement blocks to halt bulldozers.

title: Bengal's Falta Records High Voter Turnout In Repoll As BJP Eyes Win
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari thanked the people of Falta and said visuals from the constituency resembled a festival.

title: First International Big Cat Alliance Summit In Delhi Postponed
content: The summit was being organised in conjunction with the 4th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS IV) as several African countries host big cats and are important partners in global efforts towards conservation, ecological sustainability and biodiversity protection.

title: "Forget Past, Focus On Future": PM Modi's Message To Ministers At Marathon Meet
content: "Forget what happened in the past, focus on the future," PM Modi told ministers during the closed-door meeting, sources said.

title: Iran Executes Two Men Over Armed Rebellion Charges, Links To 'Separatists'
content: Since the start of the conflict with Israel and the United States in February, Iran has ramped up executions, with many of the convicts hanged over anti-government protests that took place across Iran early this year.

title: SpaceX's IPO Bonus For Musk Has One Condition: A Million Humans On Mars
content: Musk's bonus is contingent on SpaceX's stock market value hitting targets ranging from $400 billion to $6 trillion -- along with the company moving a million people to Mars.

title: "Ridiculous, Unnecessary": Trump Eases Biden-Era Greenhouse Gas Curbs
content: Donald Trump on Thursday announced the easing of curbs on a group of powerful greenhouse gases commonly found in refrigerators and air conditioners.

title: $50 Tickets For 1,000 New Yorkers: Mamdani's World Cup-Worthy FIFA Deal
content: The tickets are currently the cheapest available through FIFA's official sales process. Fans selected through the ballot will also receive free round-trip bus transport to the stadium.

title: Muslim Side Approaches Top Court Challenging Order In Bhojshala Complex Case
content: In a huge win for the Hindu side, the Madhya Pradesh High Court declared that the disputed Bhojshala complex was a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati.

title: 'Got The Hook, Need To Build On It': Parle Official On Viral Melody Clip
content: "I think that's a great opportunity that we have got and we need to build on it," Mayank Pravinchandra Shah, Vice President at Parle Products, said about the Melody toffee viral content

title: Displaced By War, Lebanese Grandmother Feeds Thousands Of Fellow Refugees
content: The ongoing war between Israel and Hezbollah displaced over one million people in the tiny country during months of cross-border fighting.

title: Twisha Sharma's Mother-In-Law Goes To Court, Says Clips, Chats Fabricated
content: Giribala Singh has also sought that the cellphones belonging to Twisha Sharma's family be seized and sent for forensic investigation.

title: 'Cockroach Janta Party': Social Media Frenzy Or Just Hype?
content: The massive influx of followers for 'Cockroach Janta Party' has triggered scrutiny, with many pointing to potential bot activity from abroad.

title: "Didn't Like Certain Aspects": Trump Postpones Signing AI Order
content: The order would create a voluntary framework for AI developers to engage with the US government before the public release of covered models

title: Maruti Suzuki To Hike Prices Across Models By Up To Rs 30,000 From June
content: In January this year, the company came out with a price protection scheme for those customers who booked their vehicles, as it could not supply due to production constraints.

title: West Asia crisis hurting Indian tea exporters
content: According to the Indian Tea Association, about 46% of the country’s tea exports are concentrated in Iran, the UAE, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye, and Egypt

title: From rallies to reels: political catchphrases turn dance anthems in Kolkata
content: Why are Kolkata club-goers dancing to Mamata Banerjee’s ‘Hamba Hamba’ and the BJP’s ‘Mach Chor’ ? From campaign songs and election slogans to viral reels and packed dancefloors, Bengal’s political techno remixes are blurring the lines between propaganda, memes and nightlife

title: Supreme Court declines to interfere with forest clearances for Adani’s Madhya Pradesh coal block project
content: The petitioner argued that a technical objection on the ground of delay ought not to preclude the court from examining the validity of the clearances

title: Sedition trials can go on if accused is willing: Supreme Court
content: In May 2022, the top court put on hold trials in sedition cases until the government completed its promised exercise “to re-examine and re-consider” the colonial-era provision of Section 124A

title: Himachal urban poll results reflect public confidence in Congress; party united with no groupism, says CM Sukhu
content: Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu said the Congress party has fulfilled all the promises made during the 2022 Assembly elections and has gone beyond its guarantees in delivering its commitments

title: Bangladesh-China talks underway for Tarique Rahman’s first foreign visit
content: Bangladesh may raise the need for infrastructure development, the relocation of Chinese industries to Bangladesh, the establishment of Chinese economic zones in Bangladesh, Prothom Alo reports

title: Bihar midday meal incident: HC asks whether NGO can continue providing meals
content: The court directed the Saharsa Superintendent of Police, the Food Safety Officer and PM Poshan officials to file affidavits explaining the seizure, sampling and laboratory examination processes.

title: Take forward reforms, PM tells Ministers
content: The meeting of full Council of Ministers was held in the backdrop of U.S.-Iran war and difficult economic situation that could further result from the event

title: New govt. hospital, preventing waterlogging are among my priorities, says Katpadi MLA
content: M. Sudhakar of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) says he has been in constant touch with Vellore Corporation to address civic concerns and formulate a long-term plan for Katpadi constituency

title: Ladakh leaders to meet Home Ministry officials on May 22, Sonam Wangchuk part of delegation
content: This is the first such meeting of members of the Leh Apex Body (LAB) and the Kargil Democratic Alliance (KDA), the two bodies representing Leh and Kargil districts respectively, since February 4; the KDA and LAB have been demanding constitutional safeguards such as Statehood and inclusion of Ladakh under the Sixth Schedule

title: Manipur DGP moved to Cabinet Secretariat
content: Rajiv Singh has been appointed Secretary (Security) in the Cabinet Secretariat. He had been appointed as Manipur’s police chief by relaxing service conditions after an ethnic violence erupted in the State on May 3, 2023.

title: Summer showers, strong winds damage over 200 houses in Ernakulam
content: Heavy rain and gusty winds battered eastern regions, including Aluva and Kothamangalam, during the third week of April. In Ernakulam district, crops on 2,306.62 hectares were damaged between March 1 and May 18 this year

title: GCDA market revamp spells tough times for traders ahead of monsoon
content: Mostly fish, meat, and chicken vendors have shifted to makeshift shacks outside the market after being asked to vacate over a month ago on the assurance that they will be reinstated before monsoon

title: KMRL offers measures to ease traffic snarls as monsoon nears
content: Additional traffic wardens will be deployed and safety measures will be stepped up along the Kakkanad route as part of monsoon preparedness amid ongoing Phase II work

title: Indian Union Muslim League’s makeover from an ally to an electoral powerhouse in Kerala
content: The Indian Union Muslim League, the second-largest constituent in the Congress-led United Democratic Front in Kerala, has socially and politically refashioned itself to emerge as a significant player in the State while scripting its political success. Ultimately, the party has found its role extended far beyond the 22 seats, its best electoral performance in the Kerala State Assembly elections . Abdul Latheef Naha tracks the transformation of the party, which has a decisive presence in the Malabar region

title: Historic shift: Vijay’s Cabinet marks Tamil Nadu’s best SC representation till date
content: This is the first time that seven people from the SC community have been included simultaneously in the Tamil Nadu Cabinet

title: DMK faces a challenge as allies set to join TVK government
content: While the Congress abandoned the DMK soon after polls and joined Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay’s Cabinet on Thursday, the IUML and VCK are set to join the Ministry on Friday, signalling the end of their relations with the DMK

title: States should be allowed to conduct CET for medical courses: CM
content: The Prime Minister and the Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan should bear the responsibility for the NEET question paper leak, says Siddaramaiah

title: Retired judge P.N. Desai to be Chairperson of Karnataka State Human Rights Commission
content: Desai probed the alleged land scam in Mysore Urban Development Authority in which the name of Parvathi Siddaramaiah, wife of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, had figured

title: Room for reconciliation within the AIADMK
content: A senior leader of the rebels, who include former Ministers C.Ve. Shanmugam, S.P. Velumani and G. Hari, says that efforts were on “from the beginning” to bring the two camps together

title: Indigenous Vayu Astra-1 completes high-altitude trials in Uttarakhand, Pokhran
content: Pune-based Nibe Limited completes no-cost, no-commitment demonstrations of its indigenous loitering munition system

title: Victims have long been ‘pushed into a corner’, says Supreme Court
content: The top court was hearing a plea filed by an accused in a chit fund scam seeking the clubbing of FIRs registered across multiple States

title: Karnataka government mulls legal action over SIR
content: Congress likely to convene a meeting of its legislators, defeated candidates and former legislators for a meeting on May 24

title: 2020 Delhi riots: HC grants bail to Salim Malik in UAPA case
content: The Bench notes that Malik has been in custody for more than five years and 10 months and the stage of the trial is that arguments on the charges are being presently heard. Suggesting that the trial is going to take some time, it also considers his alleged role in the case to grant him bail

title: India-Africa Forum Summit postponed in wake of Ebola virus outbreak
content: Decision taken after consultations regarding the evolving health situation, say MEA and Africa Union; India pledges solidarity, aid in an Africa-led approach; last such summit was held over a decade ago in 2015

title: Study reveals steady increase in thunderstorm and lightning activities in Bengaluru
content: Because heat and humidity are foundational building blocks for storm development, the probability of more severe localised thunderstorms is expected to rise in the coming years, says the study

title: Swearing-in ceremony in Kerala Assembly presents interesting moments as T.K. Govindan and V. Kunhikrishnan take oath
content: T.K. Govindan, who had left the CPI(M) owing to internal disagreements, surprised everyone when he strode up to Pinarayi Vijayan first before taking the oath. Pinarayi half-rose to shake Govindan’s hand

title: Mercury touches 48.1°C in Piduguralla, second highest in A.P. since 2016
content: IMD says ‘severe heatwave’ is likely in East Godavari, Konaseema, Eluru, West Godavari, NTR, Krishna, Palnadu, Guntur, Bapatla, Prakasam, and SPSR Nellore districts on May 23 and 24

title: Watch: Vande Mataram: What’s the bigger picture in West Bengal? | Above the Fold | 20.05.2026
content: In today’s episode, West Bengal politics dominates headlines as the State makes Vande Mataram compulsory in madrasas, announces action against illegal infiltrators, and restricts officials from speaking to media. Plus, the viral Cockroach Janata Party controversy, Tamil Nadu’s major Cabinet reshuffle under C. Joseph Vijay, and more

title: Northern Kerala districts grappling with shortage of Plus One seats again
content: According to the data compiled by Kozhikode-based NGO Malabar Education Movement,  the shortfall of seats is over 26,000 in Malappuram district

title: Satheesan to open State-level Pravesanotsavam on June 1
content: Government Model Girls Higher Secondary School, Pattom, to host the celebration; Minister for General Education N. Samsudheen will preside

title: Suo motu case against top Karnataka officials after Lokayukta raids expose illegal sand mining in Bagalkot
content: Director of Mines and Geology Department in Bengaluru, Deputy Director of Mines and Geology in Bagalkot, the Bagalkot Deputy Commissioner, and tahsildars of Badami, Mudhol, Jamakhandi, Hunagund and Ilkal taluks are among those named in case

title: How Kamala Harris sank: Demos admit 2024 went wrong but have no roadmap for 2028
content: the Democratic Party released its long-awaited autopsy of the disastrous 2024 presidential campaign that resulted in Kamala Harris losing the election to Donald Trump . the 192-page review was intended to explain how Harris lost the election despite massive fundraising, a late burst of enthusiasm after president Joe Biden exited the race . instead, the report has exposed a party still deeply divided over what exactly went wrong .

title: Wangchuk set to join Ladakh talks today
content: Sonam Wangchuk will take part in talks with the Centre on ladakh . LAB is an umbrella group of the region’s political and religious organisations . lakrook is co-chairman of LAB and president of Ladakh Buddhist Association .

title: Council of Ministers meeting: PM signals fresh reform push, focus on ease of living
content: the council of ministers led by NITI Aayog member spent over four hours taking stock of recommendations of a committee led by PM Narendra Modi . the discussions happened amid a clear message from PM Modi that govt shouldn’t interfere in people's lives and should instead come to their help when they need it .

title: Indians still receive highest number of long-term UK visas among non-EU arrivals
content: around 40,000 work, 89,000 study and 9,000 other long-term visas were issued to Indian citizens in the year ending Dec 2025, totalling 138,000 arrivals . the other nationalities in the top five were Pakistani, Chinese, Nigerian and Nepalese .

title: No rationing of fuel, bulk consumers leading to long queues: Govt
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. title: Calcutta HC to CBI: Set up SIT to probe RG Kar probe lapses & 'hush-up' bid
content: the court is hearing a plea filed by the parents of the RG Kar rape-murder victim . it directed the central bureau of investigation to form a three-member special investigation team . the alleged "lapses" by the state police and hospital authorities during the initial investigation and alleged destruction of evidence .

title: Cow slaughter not part of Eid or Islam: Calcutta HC
content: the BJP govt's order mentions the west Bengal Animal Slaughter Control Act, 1950, and a 2018 HC order . the order mandates the prohibition of cow and buffalo slaughter unless a certificate is obtained that the animal is fit for slaughter . HC refused to quash the order regulating cattle slaughter before the festival or grant interim relief based on a batch of PILs challenging the administration's directive .

title: 'Suspicious act' led to Howrah train fire, shows CCTV footage
content: security agencies and RPF obtained footage from Howrah station . the footage allegedly captures a suspect throwing an object into a coach . it triggered a fire and smoke inside a compartment of the train .

content: the deceased were identified as Anil Harkesh Chauhan, 22, and his younger brother . their uncle, Arjun Surajbali, 26, is undergoing treatment at a private hospital . police suspect a fallout of an old rivalry and extortion dispute .

title: SC: Even PMLA accused has a right to be heard
content: a bench of justices rejected the plea of Enforcement Directorate . the BNSS law is a standalone enactment and the provisions of the law had no application to proceedings under it . we hold that the aforementioned proviso is substantive in nature, as it does not merely regulate the manner in which the proceedings are to be conducted .

title: AI plane’s tail scrapes runway while going around at Bengaluru
content: a Boeing 747 jumbo jet had taken off before this flight from Delhi was to land . pilots opt for go-around to avoid getting caught in the turbulence after touchdown . the aircraft came back to safely land on the second attempt and has now been grounded for checks .

title: Does 1950 pact give Nepal citizens right to settle in India?: HC
content: govt has not placed on record what privileges Indian nationals received in Nepal . a division bench of chief justice manoj Kumar Gupta and justice subhash Upadhyay raised the issue while hearing a PIL filed by Nainital resident Pawan Jatav against the Union of India . the petitioner alleged that around 25 families from Nepal had occupied the land without Indian citizenship .

title: Assam MLAs take oath in 9 languages
content: Kangkan Kalita is a reporter with The Times of India and covers issues on health, education, stories of human interest while keeping a close watch on political developments and student movements. Reporting on environment and forest related issues and concerns of the northeast interest him equally. Read More

title: Pulwama attack planner Hamza Burhan killed by unknown gunmen in POK
content: Raj Shekhar Jha is a journalist for the Times of India with over a decade of experience in reporting on national security, terrorism, crime and prisons.

title: Vijay expands TN cabinet with 7 Dalit mins; Congress back in govt after 59 years
content: TN now has 33 ministers . CM C Joseph Vijay inducted seven Dalit ministers, the highest representation for the community in TN’s history . the number is expected to rise to eight once VCK joins the ministry .

title: CBDT detects very rich ‘farmers’ without land
content: authorities used satellite maps to detect misuse of tax provision . over 300 cases have been identified by the authorities . exempt income claimed was between Rs 50 lakh and Rs 400 crore . the declared size of land holdings was zero .

title: NEET paper leak row: CBI grills doctor, teacher, parlour owner
content: education consultancy operator Shubham Khairnar, suspended junior college teacher manisha Mandhare, beauty parlour owner Manisha Waghmare and a latur-based paediatrician moved centre stage . a growing list of suspected beneficiaries are chasing money trails and digital footprints . over 22 lakh students wrote NEET-UG, which was cancelled on may 12 following allegations of widespread malpractice .

title: Muslim side in SC, challenges high court's Bhojshala order
content: the disputed Bhojshala complex is a temple dedicated to Saraswati . the mosque's caretaker, Qazi Moinuddin, has challenged HC's order on may 15 . for the first time in 23 years, namaz will not be offered at the complex .

title: Metamorphosis: ‘Cockroach’ grows into giant on social media
content: At The Times of India, Ismat Ara covers politics, people, and societies, with a focus on accountability, justice, and lived experience. Shaped by years of rigorous reporting across cities, small towns, and remote villages, she is drawn to the human stories tucked beneath policy and paperwork. Read More

title: Ebola outbreak forces India to put off Africa summit
content: the 4th India-Africa summit that India was to host from May 28 to 31 was postponed . this is the second time that an outbreak has led to postponement of the summit . the last summit India hosted was originally scheduled for Dec 2014 but could take place only 10 months later .

title: Fire scare on B'luru-Delhi Air India flight: Pilots declare full emergency; all onboard safe
content: the aircraft landed safely at IGI airport’s runway 29R at 9.30 pm . all the about 180 people on board are learnt to be safe . a DGCA team has begun its probe into the incident . the pilots received a fire warning from one of the engines .
